sector_rotation.explanation: No clear rotation signal. Cyclical and defensive sectors are performing similarly — market may be in a transitional phase.

news_headlines.description: Delta Air Lines has announced that a next-generation Delta One product is coming to its newest plane next year. Retrofits are also in the works for its older aircraft cabins.

news_headlines.title: Microsoft Plots New Copilot Features Inspired by OpenClaw - The Information
news_headlines.description: As Microsoft faces growing competition for business customers from Anthropic, it is developing new features that would make its AI assistant Copilot function more like OpenClaw, the open-source AI agent that has taken the AI industry by storm in recent months…

news_headlines.title: Roblox defends expanded age‑checks after parents raise concerns over errors - BBC
news_headlines.description: With 144m daily users, the gaming company is extending its tech to introduce two age‑specific accounts.

news_headlines.description: Revolution Medicines' drug for pancreatic cancer succeeded in a highly anticipated Phase 3 trial.

news_headlines.title: Iran war’s global energy crisis sharpens China’s advantage in clean tech - AP News
news_headlines.description: The war in Iran is sharpening China’s advantage in promoting a renewable energy future, as oil disruptions expose the fragility of fossil fuel systems. Higher fuel prices are accelerating interest in electric vehicles and other clean technologies across Asia.…

news_headlines.title: Allogene Therapeutics Reports Interim Futility Analysis from Pivotal ALPHA3 Trial Showing 58.3% MRD Clearance with Cemacabtagene Ansegedleucel (Cema-Cel) vs. 16.7% in Observation Arm in First-Line Consolidation LBCL - Allogene
news_headlines.description: 41.6% Absolute Difference in the Cema-Cel Arm Over the Observation Arm Exceeded Clinically Meaningful Benchmark Based on Literature of 25-30% MRD Reduction Occurred Rapidly Following Cema-Cel Treatment with a 97.7% Median Decrease in Plasma ctDNA at Day 45 Co…

news_headlines.title: Ineos Automotive: Startup backed by a knighted billionaire and soccer mogul wants to rekindle the rugged SUV market - CNBC
news_headlines.description: The automaker has plans to grow sales in the U.S. by roughly 30% to 35%, as well as a target to achieve breakeven this year, executives exclusively told CNBC.

news_headlines.description: Equities unit powers bank’s earnings, while fixed income, currencies and commodities business fell well short

news_headlines.description: "It is too soon to identify potential second-round effects in inflation or labor markets," cautioned UBS's Paul Donovan.

news_headlines.title: 102 years ago, a California teenager changed American food history - SFGATE
news_headlines.description: Colorado Boulevard, a historic Pasadena stretch of California's Route 66, is home to a plaque marking the creation of the cheeseburger.

news_headlines.description: The owner of Instagram and Facebook has fueled growth with new ad products and AI

news_headlines.description: Bankers and bank regulators are scrambling to figure out what to do.

news_headlines.title: Global Bonds Slide as Failure of Talks Adds to Inflation Fears - Bloomberg.com
news_headlines.description: Treasuries slipped as oil prices spiked following US President Donald Trump’s threat to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

nyt_headlines.title: Middle East War Will Slow Global Economic Growth, I.M.F. Warns
nyt_headlines.abstract: The conflict could also fuel another bout of inflation, according to the International Monetary Fund.

nyt_headlines.title: What’s Driving the Wartime Stock Rebound
nyt_headlines.abstract: The S&P 500 has recouped all its losses since the Middle East war started on Feb. 28, as a bullish “vibe shift” takes hold among some investors.

nyt_headlines.title: Oil Prices Slip and Stocks Gain as Investors Eye Possible Path to Peace
nyt_headlines.abstract: Investors saw a possible path to peace, with the United States and Iran trading proposals for Iran to suspend nuclear activity.

nyt_headlines.title: Trump’s Latest Oil Blockade Brings Bigger Economic Risks
nyt_headlines.abstract: Oil markets shrugged it off, but the effort to hurt Iran could provoke retaliation that inflicts more damage on energy assets and the global economy.

nyt_headlines.title: Can Michigan Become the U.S. Drone Capital?
nyt_headlines.abstract: The state, known for making cars, is spending millions to entice drone manufacturers that can bring new business to auto suppliers.

nyt_headlines.title: China Imposes New Rules to Block Foreign Companies From ‘Decoupling’
nyt_headlines.abstract: Multinationals in China are concerned that the regulations could allow authorities to penalize companies and executives for shifting supply chains away from the country.

nyt_headlines.title: Netflix Leader Makes Rare Overture to Cinema Owners
nyt_headlines.abstract: Ted Sarandos attended a major movie theater conference for the first time and met with domestic and international owners, people familiar with the meetings said.

nyt_headlines.title: Two Iran-linked Ships Passed Through Strait of Hormuz Ahead of U.S. Blockade
nyt_headlines.abstract: The vessels exited the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, hours before a U.S. naval blockade took effect.

nyt_headlines.title: Shipping Companies Remain Reluctant to Sail Through Strait of Hormuz
nyt_headlines.abstract: The United States said it has started to block vessels doing business with Iran from the strait. Details on how that would work are unclear.

nyt_headlines.title: High Gas Prices Won’t End Even if the War Does
nyt_headlines.abstract: Why a cease-fire in Iran won’t bring gasoline prices down.

nyt_headlines.title: Investors Brace for a New Hormuz Blockade Threat
nyt_headlines.abstract: President Trump’s plan to bar ships from entering or leaving Iranian ports has put markets on edge and added to global economic uncertainty.
